description: Productize ReAgent into a publicly adoptable open-source research workspace with Hermes-style runtime seams, safe backup and recovery flows, release-grade verification, and clear developer extension contracts.

userStories.title: Publish a stable public install guide and quickstart
userStories.description: As an open-source evaluator, I want one official install path so that I can get ReAgent running without reading source code.
acceptanceCriteria: README highlights one recommended install path using `@sinlair/reagent`
Quickstart covers Node version, database initialization, runtime start, and Web URL
Install failure guidance points to `reagent onboard` or `reagent doctor`
Quickstart includes one end-to-end path from an empty directory to a running Web console
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tighten the first-run flow across onboard, home, and the Web console
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want onboard, home, and the Web console to form one guided first-run path so that I do not get dropped into implementation details.
acceptanceCriteria: `reagent onboard` next steps match `reagent home` and first-run Web copy
Starter profile, channel mode, and runtime health are described consistently across CLI and Web
First-run surfaces point to the next action without requiring manual command discovery
The first-run path covers `reagent onboard`, `reagent home`, `reagent service run`, and Web console entry
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create workspace backup snapshots
userStories.description: As a long-term user, I want one command to create a workspace snapshot so that I can safely upgrade, migrate, or experiment without losing work.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a workspace backup snapshot command
Snapshot covers file-backed memory, channel runtime state, research rounds, and the database layer
Successful backup returns one concrete local snapshot path
Backup does not overwrite an existing snapshot by default
Snapshot metadata records creation time and included content
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preview workspace restore before applying it
userStories.description: As a user restoring a workspace, I want to preview a snapshot before applying it so that I do not accidentally replace my current state.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a restore preview command
Preview shows snapshot time, included content, and target workspace path
Preview does not modify the active workspace
Preview reports missing or unreadable snapshot content clearly
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Apply workspace restore safely
userStories.description: As a user restoring a workspace, I want restore apply to require explicit confirmation so that recovery does not silently destroy current data.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a restore apply command
Restore apply requires explicit confirmation or an equivalent safety flag
Restore apply can repopulate `reagent home`, recent research, and memory files from restored data
Restore failures preserve a debuggable error state instead of partially overwriting the workspace silently
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Export a support bundle without leaking secrets
userStories.description: As an open-source user hitting a problem, I want to export a support bundle so that I can debug locally or attach it to an issue without leaking plaintext secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a support bundle export command
Bundle includes runtime status, doctor summary, recent logs, channel lifecycle, and agent runtime audit
Plaintext secrets are excluded by default
Successful export returns one concrete local bundle path
Bundle output is suitable for local diagnosis or issue attachment
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add product-facing recovery hints to key surfaces
userStories.description: As a user blocked by an error, I want key product surfaces to show recovery hints so that I know what to do next without reading raw logs.
acceptanceCriteria: Home, Settings, Channels, and Agents surfaces show product-facing recovery hints for blocking failures
Recovery hints name the next command, next setting, or next diagnostic artifact to inspect
Hints do not expose raw internal stack traces by default
Recovery hint wording is consistent with CLI doctor and status output
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add browser smoke verification to release checks
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want release verification to run a browser smoke gate so that broken public builds are blocked before release.
acceptanceCriteria: Release verification includes a browser smoke flow
Smoke covers Landing or Home, Sessions, Agents, session switching, and history, hooks, runtime, and delegations panels
Smoke failures emit debuggable artifacts such as screenshots or result JSON
Smoke runs in a mock environment without real WeChat pairing
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create a stable public docs hub and navigation entrypoints
userStories.description: As an open-source user or contributor, I want a structured docs entrypoint so that I can quickly find the right documentation without reading one long README.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a docs landing page or docs index linked from README
Docs navigation separates user, developer, and operations entrypoints
README no longer serves as the only documentation surface for all audiences
Core docs entrypoints are valid repo-relative links
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document core product and runtime flows
userStories.description: As a user or operator, I want docs for the core product flows so that I can install, run, upgrade, recover, and inspect ReAgent without guessing the intended path.
acceptanceCriteria: Docs cover install, run, upgrade, recovery, research, memory, agent runtime, and OpenClaw bridge flows
Each flow page names the primary commands or pages to use
Docs distinguish everyday user actions from maintainer or operator actions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Publish a workspace skill example and contract guide
userStories.description: As a third-party developer, I want one runnable workspace skill example and contract guide so that I can extend ReAgent without reverse-engineering internal code.
acceptanceCriteria: Add one runnable workspace skill example
Example documents required files, configuration, and validation steps
Docs distinguish stable skill integration fields from unstable implementation details
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Publish an MCP example and contract guide
userStories.description: As a third-party developer, I want one MCP example and contract guide so that I can integrate external tools into ReAgent with clear expectations.
acceptanceCriteria: Add one MCP server configuration example
Example documents required config, expected behavior, and validation steps
Docs distinguish stable MCP integration fields from unstable implementation details
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Publish a plugin or bridge example and contract guide
userStories.description: As a third-party developer, I want one plugin or bridge-facing example and contract guide so that I can understand how ReAgent extends beyond built-in workspace skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Add one plugin-facing or bridge-facing example
Example documents required contract fields, configuration, and validation steps
Docs distinguish stable plugin or bridge integration fields from unstable implementation details
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Publish public product examples and walkthroughs
userStories.description: As a first-time evaluator, I want a few high-quality walkthroughs so that I can quickly understand ReAgent's product value before committing to adoption.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide at least one personal researcher walkthrough
Provide at least one OpenClaw or channel runtime inspection walkthrough
Provide at least one brief-to-report-to-artifact-reopen walkthrough
Example commands and screenshots match current CLI or Web behavior
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document release, compatibility, and positioning rules
userStories.description: As a maintainer or contributor, I want explicit release, compatibility, and product-positioning rules so that users know what is stable and how ReAgent differs from Hermes-Agent.
acceptanceCriteria: Docs mark stable surfaces, compatibility aliases, and experimental features
Release process documents changelog expectations, upgrade notes, and breaking-change handling
README or docs explicitly state that ReAgent is a research-first workspace rather than a completed general-purpose agent runtime
Docs explain shared Hermes-style runtime seams versus ReAgent-specific research artifact and memory models
Typecheck passes
